title: Identify Why A Tote Was Sent To The Hospital Station
summary: Use the hospital station stacklight and green scanner to confirm a tote is present and determine the reason it was brought to the hospital station.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a tote has been sent to the hospital station and the operator needs to confirm tote presence and determine why the tote was brought there for manual handling.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to interpret scanner codes, status values, or HMI fields beyond determining the reason, because the source does not provide those details.
Do not use this runbook as a complete tote handling, removal, add-back, or manual bagging procedure.

responsibilities: Confirm tote presence using the station indication described in the source.
Use the green scanner to determine why the tote was brought to the station.
Communicate or record the reason for manual handling.

safety_notes: The source states the tote is manually handled by an operator at the hospital station.
Do not invent or apply unsupported follow-up handling steps, scanner interpretations, or controls not described in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the hospital station stacklight

access_required: Operator access to the hospital station
system_preconditions: A tote has been directed to the hospital station by the WCS for manual handling.
The AGV has delivered or is expected to deliver the tote to the hospital station.

visual_references.description: Photo of the hospital station for station identification and stacklight context.
Hospital station operation artifact showing or accompanying the procedure for handling totes at a hospital station.
Example hospital station screen associated with one reason a tote may be sent to the hospital station.
Example hospital station screen associated with barcode mismatch handling.
Example hospital station screen associated with tote failed to empty handling.
Example hospital station screen associated with a sent-from-bagging-station condition.

steps.title: Go to the hospital station and check for tote presence indication
steps.purpose: Verify that the hospital station is the active location for the tote requiring manual handling.
steps.instruction: Go to the hospital station and observe the station indication that shows a tote is present.
steps.expected_result: The operator identifies whether the station indicates a tote is present.
steps.healthy_condition: A tote-present indication is observed at the hospital station.
steps.failure_condition: No tote-present indication is observed when a tote is expected at the hospital station.

actions.label: Observe hospital station indication
actions.exact_action: Go to the hospital station and observe the stacklight that indicates a tote is present.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the hospital station photo to identify the station and its stacklight context.
Use the hospital station operation artifact as supporting visual context for hospital station tote handling.

stop_or_escalate_if: The station does not indicate a tote is present when one is expected.
The observed station condition does not match the expected hospital station condition.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: A stacklight indicates a tote is present. An operator uses a scanner (green scanner) to determine the reason the tote was brought to the station.
Figure 3-5 appears on page 29 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_3_3_1_hospital_station_stacklight.

steps.title: Confirm the tote is on the operator-right side of the hospital station
steps.purpose: Verify the tote is positioned where the source says the AGV delivers it at the hospital station.
steps.instruction: Confirm the AGV has brought the tote to the right side of the hospital station from the operator's perspective.
steps.expected_result: The operator confirms the tote is positioned on the right side of the hospital station.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote is present on the right side of the hospital station from the operator's perspective.
steps.failure_condition: The tote is not on the expected side or the tote location cannot be confirmed.

actions.label: Verify tote position
actions.exact_action: Confirm the AGV has brought the tote to the right side of the hospital station from the operator's perspective.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the hospital station operation artifact for visual context on AGV tote delivery and station sides.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote is not on the right side of the hospital station from the operator's perspective.
The tote presence or position does not match the expected hospital station condition.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The AGV brings the tote to the right side (based on the perspective of the operator) of the hospital station.

steps.title: Use the green scanner to determine the reason for hospital station routing
steps.purpose: Identify why the tote was brought to the hospital station for manual handling.
steps.instruction: Use the green scanner at the hospital station to determine the reason the tote was brought to the station.
steps.expected_result: The scanner provides the reason the tote was brought to the hospital station.
steps.healthy_condition: A reason for the tote being sent to the hospital station is obtained using the green scanner.
steps.failure_condition: The scanner does not provide a reason or the reason cannot be determined from the available source-supported interaction.

actions.label: Use green scanner
actions.exact_action: Use the green scanner at the hospital station to determine the reason the tote was brought to the station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use as an example of a hospital station exception screen related to a no-read barcode condition.
Use as an example of a hospital station exception screen related to barcode mismatch.
Use as an example of a hospital station exception screen related to tote failed to empty.
Use as an example of a hospital station screen associated with a sent-from-bagging-station condition.

stop_or_escalate_if: The scanner does not provide a reason.
The source-supported scanner interaction is insufficient to determine the reason.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: A stacklight indicates a tote is present. An operator uses a scanner (green scanner) to determine the reason the tote was brought to the station.
Figure 4-33 appears on page 64 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_4_1_3_2_4_1_3_no_read_bar_code.
Figure 4-34 appears on page 65 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_4_1_3_2_5_1_4_bar_code_mismatch.

steps.title: Record or communicate the reported reason
steps.purpose: Ensure the identified reason is available for manual handling or escalation.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate the reason reported by the scanner for manual handling of the tote.
steps.expected_result: The reason is captured or communicated for the next action outside this runbook.
steps.healthy_condition: The scanner-reported reason is clearly recorded or communicated.
steps.failure_condition: The reason cannot be recorded or communicated because it was not obtained or is unclear.

actions.label: Capture reported reason
actions.exact_action: Record or communicate the reason reported by the scanner for manual handling of the tote.

stop_or_escalate_if: The scanner-reported reason is unavailable or unclear.
Follow-up handling requires details not provided by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: A stacklight indicates a tote is present. An operator uses a scanner (green scanner) to determine the reason the tote was brought to the station.

success_criteria: The operator confirms a tote is present at the hospital station.
The operator confirms the tote is on the right side of the hospital station from the operator's perspective.
The operator determines the reason the tote was brought to the hospital station using the green scanner.
The reason is recorded or communicated for manual handling.
healthy_conditions: Hospital station indicates a tote is present.
The tote is positioned on the right side of the hospital station from the operator's perspective.
The green scanner provides a reason for the tote being sent to the station.
failure_conditions: The hospital station does not indicate a tote is present when expected.
The tote is not on the expected side of the hospital station.
The green scanner does not provide a reason.
The source does not provide enough detail to continue with interpretation or follow-up handling.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the scanner does not provide a reason.
Escalate if tote presence does not match the expected hospital station condition.
Escalate if follow-up handling depends on scanner screens, codes, or interpretation details not provided in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital station photo and stacklight context.
Hospital station operation visual context for tote handling.
Example exception screen for no-read barcode condition.
Example exception screen for barcode mismatch condition.
Example exception screen for tote failed to empty condition.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: In the event of a mis-sort, bad tip, or tote overload, the AGV is directed by the WCS to take the tote to a hospital station to be manually handled by an operator.
A stacklight indicates a tote is present. An operator uses a scanner (green scanner) to determine the reason the tote was brought to the station.
The AGV brings the tote to the right side (based on the perspective of the operator) of the hospital station.

missing_details: The source does not provide scanner screens, scanner codes, or exact scanner interaction details for determining the reason.
The source does not define the exact stacklight state to use for this specific tote-present confirmation in this procedure.
The source does not provide required follow-up handling steps after the reason is identified.
The source does not provide a formal recording method, destination, or communication channel for the identified reason.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
